title: Indicators | Stats NZ
content:
## Change in use of Māori land for primary production
Land use and the proportions of the main livestock on Māori farms changed between 2006 and 2016.

## Wetland extent – published April 2018
By 2008, wetlands had declined to 10.1 percent (about 250,000 ha) of the estimated pre-human extent of 2,471,080 ha – especially swamps.

## Estimated long-term soil erosion – published April 2018
In 2012, it was estimated that 192 million tonnes of eroded soil entered New Zealand’s rivers each year; of this, an estimated 84 million tonnes (44 percent) was from exotic grassland.

## Annual glacier ice volumes – published January 2018
There was a trend of decreasing glacier ice volume between 1977 and 2016. Glacier ice volume decreased 25 percent from 1977 to 2016.

## Global greenhouse gas emissions
Greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions trap heat in the atmosphere and warm the planet.

## Interdecadal Pacific Oscillation
The Interdecadal Pacific Oscillation (IPO) was in a positive phase during 2014–16 and a negative phase during 1999–2013.

## Southern Annular Mode
The Southern Annular Mode (SAM) has been increasing (becoming more positive) since 1970.

## Occurrence of food- and water-borne diseases
Climate is not the only influence on infection rates (food- and water-borne diseases can occur at any time of year), but it is an important factor.

## Global production of ozone-depleting substances
From 1986 to 2015 global ODS production fell 98 percent. There were decreases in the production of chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), halons, other fully halogenated CFCs, carbon tetrachloride, methyl chloroform, and methyl bromide throughout this period.

## UV intensity – published October 2017
New Zealand’s UV levels are variable and we experience both very high and very low UV levels, depending on the time of year.
